topics.title: Brain Aging and Alzheimer Risk Markers
topics.description: This cluster centers on aging-related cognitive decline and neurodegenerative disease, especially Alzheimer disease, mild cognitive impairment, and dementia risk. It also highlights structural and biomarker measures such as brain age, amyloid burden, and MRI-based changes as early markers of vulnerability.

topics.title: Functional Connectivity Reliability Across Individuals
topics.description: This cluster focuses on functional connectivity and brain network organization, especially how resting-state and dynamic measures vary across individuals and conditions. A major emphasis is on reliability, stability, reproducibility, and precision mapping in human brain imaging.

topics.title: Deep Learning MRI Segmentation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI segmentation and reconstruction approaches, especially automated pipelines using deep learning and foundation models. It also includes work on robustness, denoising, and adapting tools across field strengths and imaging protocols.

topics.title: Naturalistic fMRI and Neural Representation
topics.description: Studies of naturalistic fMRI examine how attention, working memory, curiosity, and visual imagery shape neural representations and neural dynamics in the human brain. The cluster emphasizes functional organization and neural correlates during movie watching and related cognitive tasks.

topics.title: Brain Development Across Early Life Stages
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain development and developmental trajectories from infancy through childhood and adolescence, with emphasis on neonatal and preterm populations. Common themes include white matter, cortical folding, and cognitive outcomes across early life.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how language and speech are represented and processed in the brain across development and lifespan. It includes auditory and reading-related functions, comprehension, and neural correlates in multilingual and clinical populations.

topics.title: Affective Regulation and Anxiety Circuits
topics.description: This cluster centers on emotional and fear-related processes in anxiety, depression, PTSD, and related conditions. It emphasizes amygdala-linked neural correlates and strategies such as cognitive reappraisal and emotion regulation.

topics.title: EEG Dynamics in Cognition and Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based markers of brain dynamics, including oscillations, microstates, coupling, and signal complexity. The studies relate these measures to cognition, consciousness, interoception, and neuropsychiatric or neurodegenerative disorders.

topics.title: Neuropsychiatric Disorders and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on major psychiatric conditions including obsessive compulsive disorder, depression, and anxiety disorder, with emphasis on treatment resistant depression and related symptom response. Many abstracts also examine brain activity, functional connectivity, and fmri measures across interventions such as cbt and tms.

topics.title: Genetics and Subtypes of Depressive Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related psychiatric disorders, with emphasis on major depressive disorder, adolescent depression, and suicidal ideation. It also highlights genetic architecture and genome wide analysis in large biobank datasets such as UK Biobank.

topics.title: Motor Recovery and Brain Function After Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on stroke-related motor control, action observation, imagery, and functional recovery, often linking behavioral outcomes to lesion effects and brain organization. It also includes studies using TMS and fMRI to probe primary motor cortex function and structural disconnection.

topics.title: Autism Neurodevelopment Across Childhood and Adolescence
topics.description: The cluster focuses on autism-related neurodevelopmental differences from childhood through adolescence, including symptom expression and broader psychopathology. It emphasizes developmental timing and brain abnormalities in autistic youth and related traits.

topics.title: Reward and Craving in Addiction Neurocircuitry
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and behavioral mechanisms underlying craving, reward processing, and decision making in addiction and related disorders. It includes fMRI studies of cue reactivity and inhibitory control across alcohol, cannabis, and adolescent substance-related phenotypes.

topics.title: Diffusion MRI of White Matter Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI measures of white matter microstructure across the lifespan, with emphasis on age-related change and clinical variation. Studies examine how DTI-derived markers such as FA and callosal FA relate to brain injury, cognitive status, and neurodegenerative aging.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Spectrum Biomarkers and Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and psychosis across the spectrum, with emphasis on early psychosis, familial high risk, and related clinical subtypes. It also highlights biomarker discovery, neuroanatomical variation, and heterogeneity, including treatment response and sex differences.

topics.title: Epilepsy Localization and Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic regions and characterizing seizure-related network dynamics across epilepsy types, especially in pediatric and focal epilepsy. It also includes multimodal imaging, EEG/SEEG-based localization, and automated analytic approaches for improving surgical and clinical outcomes.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Disorders and Neuromodulation
topics.description: The cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related synucleinopathies, including REM sleep behavior disorder, iron accumulation, and substantia nigra pathology. It also includes deep brain stimulation and lesion-based approaches for treating parkinsonian symptoms such as freezing and essential tremor.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure, data standards, and tools for converting, visualizing, and managing imaging datasets. It also reflects efforts toward reproducible workflows, consortium-based coordination, and automation in clinical and research settings.

topics.title: Sleep State Dynamics and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on human sleep, especially NREM sleep structure, arousal transitions, and brain state dynamics during sleep. It also highlights aging-related changes and associated measures such as functional connectivity, coherence, and cerebrospinal fluid dynamics.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain Mechanisms in Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain conditions and how pain perception, central sensitization, and analgesia relate to brain mechanisms. It also includes specific pain syndromes and behavioral processes such as fear avoidance.

topics.title: Multiple Sclerosis Fatigue and Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue in multiple sclerosis, with emphasis on how symptoms relate to disability and multiple cognitive test scores. It also highlights multimodal neuroimaging markers, including brain volume, functional connectivity, and microstructural measures, to characterize disease phenotypes.

topics.title: Sex Hormones and Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how hormonal states and sex-related factors shape brain structure, connectivity, and lateralization across development and lifespan stages. It includes studies of menstrual, menopausal, and postpartum influences, as well as neuroendocrine and neuroanatomical sex differences.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Brain Tumor Surgery and Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain tumors and their surgical treatment alter functional connectivity, network organization, and compensatory plasticity. It also includes prediction of postoperative deficits and functional recovery, along with links to glioma grade, progression, and survival.

keywords: contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
topics.title: Sex Differences in Multimodal Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on sex differences in cognition and related brain network organization across multimodal MRI and functional connectivity studies. It also includes applications to healthy ageing and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on connectomics and structural-functional relationships.

keywords: neurochemical scaffolding of evolutionarily conserved spatiotemporal dynamics
gene expression shape brain wide response
topics.title: Neurochemical Architecture of Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neurochemical factors, receptor distributions, and gene expression shape conserved spatiotemporal dynamics across the human brain and cerebral cortex. It also includes work on inter-individual variability and receptor covariance patterns underlying brain-wide organization.

topics.title: Functional Connectivity in Brain Stimulation Targeting
topics.description: This cluster centers on functional connectivity measures used to identify reliable brain stimulation targets. It emphasizes connectivity-based approaches for understanding and guiding stimulation in neuroscience.
